atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient reports right index finger pain

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient reports right index finger pain following a skiing injury

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient reports a strap caught on his finger

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient reports a bend in the finger

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient reports initial use of ice and ibuprofen

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient reports the pain persists

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: Physical examination reveals tenderness over the distal phalanx and the joint of the right index finger

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: Range of motion is limited, with pain on flexion

atomic_propositions.speaker: test_result
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: X-rays demonstrate no fractures and normal alignment of the finger joints

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient's presentation is consistent with a right hand index finger contusion, likely involving the distal phalanx and joint
